Title: KENNEDY SPACE CENTER, FLA. -  Workers in the Space Station Processing Facility check out the Window Observational Research Facility (WORF),  designed and built by the Boeing Co. at NASA’s Marshall Space Flight Center in Huntsville, Ala.   WORF will be delivered to the International Space Station and placed in the rack position in front of the Destiny lab window,  providing locations for attaching cameras, multi-spectral scanners and other instruments. WORF will support a variety of scientific and commercial experiments in areas of Earth systems and processes, global ecological changes in Earth’s biosphere, lithosphere, hydrosphere and climate system, Earth resources, natural hazards, and education.  After installation, it will become a permanent focal point for Earth Science research aboard the space station.
CreditLine: NASA Image and Video Library (public domain)
